full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Checking if my paper satisfies the Part A rules
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Can I take this paper SVM Classifier Estimation from Group Probabilities?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Helped me confirm that the paper is from ICML 2010 and fits the allowed SVM category.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I checked the ICML venue and year myself from the paper PDF.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Is this paper reproducible on CPU using simple datasets?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: It reassured me that I can simulate grouped labels and implement it using sklearn SVR.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I saw in the experiments section that they used UCI datasets.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Trying to understand the core idea
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Explain inverse calibration in simple terms.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Gave me an intuition before I re-read the algorithm section.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I matched it with the sigmoid inverse equation in Section 3.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Understanding what experiments I could do later
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: What kind of experiments can I run for this paper?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Helped me think about testing group size and performance.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I checked the experiments section where they vary k.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Is this paper explainable for a student without heavy math?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Helped me judge that the idea is conceptual and not too theoretical.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: After reading, I felt the main idea was understandable.
